FINPROVE MEKi cohort phospho-abs for tCycIF:

FINPROVE MEKi cohort phospho-abs for tCycIF:

ORDERED ANTIBODIES:

ORDERED ANTIBODIES:

pErk: 4284S (CST)

pErk: 4284S (CST)

pATF4/CREB2: 9198S (CST)

pATF4/CREB2: 9198S (CST)

pSAPK/JNK: MA5-15228 (Thermo Fisher Scientific)

pSAPK/JNK: MA5-15228 (Thermo Fisher Scientific)

Issue here is that only pErk is available directly conjugated, and the market of phospho-antibodies is dominated by CST, leading to most antibodies being rabbit monoclonals.

Issue here is that only pErk is available directly conjugated, and the market of phospho-antibodies is dominated by CST, leading to most antibodies being rabbit monoclonals.

Background: Erk is the kinase activated by MEK, and the key signaling molecule in the classical activation of MAPK pathway. Leads to transcription factor activation

Background: Directly downstream of Erk, CREB is a transcription factor that sums various signals and results in activation of numerous downstream pathways. Highly expressed in ovarian cancer

Background: Directly downstream of Erk, CREB is a transcription factor that sums various signals and results in activation of numerous downstream pathways. Highly expressed in ovarian cancer

Background: Important mediator of stress-related signaling. Is downstream of MKK’s, which in turn can be activated by members of the Ras family, leading to an additional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway. JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.

Background: Important mediator of stress-related signaling. Is downstream of MKK’s, which in turn can be activated by members of the Ras family, leading to an additional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway. JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.

Target selection rationale

For more details on MAPK pathway map: https://www.kegg.jp/pathway/map04010

Target selection rationale

For more details on MAPK pathway map: https://www.kegg.jp/pathway/map04010

Chosen targets circled in red.

Erk is the main kinase in the MAPK cascade, and in order to quantify the overall activity of the MAPK pathway we need to assess the level of Erk activity. Additionally, the ab is available directly conjugated, so we do not use up a non-conjugated 1st cycle slot with pErk.

Chosen targets circled in red.

Erk is the main kinase in the MAPK cascade, and in order to quantify the overall activity of the MAPK pathway we need to assess the level of Erk activity. Additionally, the ab is available directly conjugated, so we do not use up a non-conjugated 1st cycle slot with pErk.

CREB2, i.e. ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g. PI3K-Akt-pathway. Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.

CREB2, i.e. ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g. PI3K-Akt-pathway. Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.

JNK is a kinase downstream for Ras, i.e. activated by components upstream of MEKi, and leads to antiapoptotic signaling. Quantifying JNK activity can give insights as to why MEKi does not lead to improved survival, as cancer cells potentially rely on other pathways to survive instead of the canonical Erk driven MAPK pathway, activated by factors upstream of MEKi, and thus leave the cancer cells able to survive MEKi.

JNK is a kinase downstream for Ras, i.e. activated by components upstream of MEKi, and leads to antiapoptotic signaling. Quantifying JNK activity can give insights as to why MEKi does not lead to improved survival, as cancer cells potentially rely on other pathways to survive instead of the canonical Erk driven MAPK pathway, activated by factors upstream of MEKi, and thus leave the cancer cells able to survive MEKi.

Additional issue is whether we can use panCK and PAX8 as cancer markers for the whole cohort. We have one endometrial stromal sarcoma, and one vaginal ADC.

Additional issue is whether we can use panCK and PAX8 as cancer markers for the whole cohort. We have one endometrial stromal sarcoma, and one vaginal ADC.

After confirming with Anni V, the vaginal ADC expresses CK7 -> panCK works for the ADC

After confirming with Anni V, the vaginal ADC expresses CK7 -> panCK works for the ADC

The endometrial stromal sarcoma requires an additional marker.

The endometrial stromal sarcoma requires an additional marker.

https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7528441/ contains info on potential markers. aSMA is expressed by ~50 % of ESS cases, not reliable enough. Transgelin, ASC1 (solute carrier family 7), Stathmin1 could be good markers. However, Stathmin 1 and ASC1 do not have conjugated antibodies available.

https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7528441/ contains info on potential markers. aSMA is expressed by ~50 % of ESS cases, not reliable enough. Transgelin, ASC1 (solute carrier family 7), Stathmin1 could be good markers. However, Stathmin 1 and ASC1 do not have conjugated antibodies available.

Transgelin has a CoraLite 488-conjugate available: https://www.thermofisher.com/antibody/product/transgelin-SM22-Antibody-Polyclonal/CL48810493100UL , if Anni V confirms the target, will order this one
